Recently, a popular "AI Workflow Cheatsheet" started circulating. So I decided to compare it with something I’ve been building over the past 6 months. Not conceptually. Not theoretically. But in real systems. What emerged is a clear distinction. Most current AI workflows are: probabilistic suggestive loosely constrained JFP takes a fundamentally different approach: deterministic execution enforced control layers truth-gated actions full auditability In simple terms: -AI suggests what to do -JFP decides if it is allowed to be done This shifts the system into a completely different category. Not an assistant. But an execution layer. One important clarification: This is not a new idea. VIPER - the tactical command layer shown below — was already running on top of JFP v7.0.0. That means JFP was never just a prompt format. From the beginning, it functioned as: a structured execution model a deterministic control layer a foundation for governed systems Since then, the architecture has evolved further into VIKI OFFICE (JFP v8.0.0). And the direction remains the same: deeper control stronger guarantees governed execution What you're seeing as “AI workflows” today… …may just be the early stage of something much bigger. AI hashtagCyberSecurity Agents ExecutionLayer DeterministicSystems ZeroTrust hashtag

Salut le mastodon C'est mercredi, tavu ? Tout va mal et on nous annonce pire. Les dits «économistes» prévoient la fin du monde pour demain et semblent y trouver un grand plaisir, sans doute ont-ils un plan pour y échapper. Qu'y pouvons nous ? à peu près rien, le monde est dirigé par des dingues qui se foutent éperdument de nous, la course à l'abîme les amuse. Alors aimons nous, prenons plaisir à contempler le ciel et les fleurs tant qu(il y en a, la vie est un don gratuit. 🤗 ✊ 🥰

[2/3] After ~2h on trail the landscape changed again, to a bleaker grassy expanse as we got nearer to the head of the valley (📷1). Water streamed off the cliffs, forming rivulets that found their way to the river, although not before leaving the ground boggy in many places (📷2). Our first new flower of the day was a native in the daisy family, Tussilago farfara | the colt’s foot (📷3). At 9km/ 3h on trail we reached the signature Cascada Cola de Caballo | horse trail waterfall, which felt somewhat anticlimactic (📷4). 🇪🇸 spain aragon 🇪🇺 europe 🚀 travel 🚐 camperVan nomad roadTrip vanLife 🥾 hiking nature nationalPark

Home - CBSNews.com | Feds have rejected 15% of businesses' tariff refund claims AI generated summary, Read the full article for complete information. Since the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) launched its new Consolidated Administration and Processing of Entries (CAPE) tariff‑refund portal on April 20, more than 75,000 refund requests have been submitted, but roughly 15 % have been rejected. The agency reports that over 47,000 claims—covering about $11 million in tariff payments—were properly filed, while many rejections appear to stem from businesses submitting claims outside the parameters allowed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act or making errors in their filings. Apart from a brief 18‑minute outage on launch day, the system has operated smoothly, a fact praised by customs officials and trade lawyers who note the rapid rollout and overall functionality of the new portal. [1/3] Pradera de Ordesa (🅿️) to the Cola de Caballo waterfall is a popular out-and-back trail in Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park | Ordesa and Monte Perdido National Park. The Senda a la Cola de Caballo trail runs beside the Río Arazas, although the first section is under forest cover with limited views (📷1). After ~100min on trail things get more open & we found ourselves admiring spring greens next to clear mountain waters & impressive walls of rock in both downstream (📷2) & upstream (📷3) directions. The trail offers a number of waterfall waypoints, but they’re not all easy to see well due to vegetation overgrowth or flooded miradors; this is part of Gradas de Soaso, a sequence of steps (📷4). 🇪🇸 spain aragon 🇪🇺 europe 🚀 travel 🚐 camperVan nomad roadTrip vanLife 🥾 hiking nature nationalPark

Modern office AU where Shen Qingqiu recognizes Luo Binghe from the homeless shelter he used to volunteer at and assumes he is the new intern, taking him under his wing amidst whispers of the CEO's son being discovered after years of searching for the woman that gave birth to him. Luo Binghe shamelessly takes full advantage and enjoys every second of the attention, leveraging his mentor into more and more hangouts completely unrelated to work. In the meantime Tianlang-Jun has been trying to set Shen Qingqiu up with his nephew and secretary for a couple of years, making Luo Binghe endlessly jealous and hissy every time Zhuzhi-Lang seeks Shen Qingqiu out with "a very important message, for your ears only." Zhuzhi-Lang is aware of his uncle's meddling but refuses to act like anything is amiss. Luo Binghe hasn't decided whether or not to drive him out into the desert and leave him there yet.

I'm currently syncing the 27,932,997 files (10.3 TB) from the wasabi to the new FediStorage bucket. Once I cut over, it will be missing a bunch of files uploaded over the last 24 hours, and take me about 12 hours for the final transfer from the old to new bucket to be completed 100%... so a random selection recent media will seem broken until the sync is completed. Details: This is a two phase sync. 99.999% completed the first pass, and the rest in the second pass. This will save me $100 a month in storage costs... and I will try to limit the cut over window to be as small as possible. This will happen sometime this week.
